name: env-manager
description: Environment variable management, validation, and documentation.
capabilities: Analyze code to identify environment variables in use
Validate environment variable formats and required values
Document environment configurations with descriptions and defaults
Provide security recommendations for secret handling
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based documentation skill containing only instructional markdown. The static analyzer flagged 188 patterns that appear in documentation examples, but all are false positives. The skill teaches legitimate environment variable management practices, explicitly recommends against committing secrets, and has no executable code, file access, network calls, or command execution capability.
valueStatement: Managing environment variables across different environments can lead to errors, security issues, and configuration headaches. This skill helps you validate, document, and secure environment configurations for any project.
limitations: Does not modify files on your filesystem
Does not execute shell commands or scripts
Does not access or read existing .env files
Does not connect to external services or APIs

useCases.title: Set up new projects
useCases.description: Generate environment configuration templates for new projects with proper validation and documentation

useCases.title: Audit configurations
useCases.description: Review environment variable usage across codebases and identify missing or misconfigured variables

useCases.title: Enforce best practices
useCases.description: Verify projects follow secret management guidelines and do not hardcode sensitive values

promptTemplates.title: Generate template
promptTemplates.scenario: Create env file for new project
promptTemplates.prompt: @env-manager Generate a .env.example template for a Node.js application with PostgreSQL, Redis, and JWT authentication

promptTemplates.title: Validate variables
promptTemplates.prompt: @env-manager What environment variables are required for a production Node.js application and how should they be validated

promptTemplates.title: Document secrets
promptTemplates.prompt: @env-manager Create documentation template for environment variables including descriptions, examples, and security notes

promptTemplates.title: Security audit
promptTemplates.prompt: @env-manager What security best practices should I follow for environment variable management in production

bestPractices: Use a validation library to check environment variables on application startup
Keep .env.example committed to version control with placeholder values
Use different configuration files for development, staging, and production environments
antiPatterns: Hardcoding secrets directly in source code
Committing .env files with real credentials to version control
Skipping validation and letting missing variables cause runtime errors

faq.question: Does this skill read my .env files?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill that provides guidance. It does not access or read any files on your system.

faq.question: What languages does it support?
faq.answer: The guidance applies to any language. Examples include Node.js, Python, and other languages using environment variables.

faq.question: Can it validate my existing configuration?
faq.answer: The skill provides validation patterns and examples. Actual validation requires integration with your application code.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill runs entirely as a prompt response and does not transmit data, access files, or execute code.

faq.question: What validation libraries are recommended?
faq.answer: For Node.js: envalid or dotenv. For Python: pydantic-settings or python-dotenv with manual validation.

faq.question: How is this different from other config management tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides conceptual guidance and templates. Tools like Docker Secrets, AWS Secrets Manager, or Vault handle actual secret storage and injection.
